title: [comb] missing peepholes:
body:
Here are some random missing peepholes seen in generated verilog from RocketCore.fir in the testsuite:

- [x] This: 

```
wire [2:0] _T_1 = {reg_debug, reg_mstatus_prv};    // reg_mstatus_prv is two bits
wire _T_57 = _T_1 > 3'h1;
```
could be: `wire _T_57 = {reg_debug, reg_mstatus_prv[1]} != 0;`

 - [x] This could simplify too: `wire _T_20 = (_T_18 & 3'h3) > 3'h1;`
---

- [x] This: `wire [63:0] _T_0 = _T ? ~io_in2 : io_in2;`

could be strength reduced to: `wire [63:0] _T_0 = sext(_T)^io_in2;`

---

 - [x] This `_T_167 = _T_51 ? ~_T_83 | _T_160 : _T_160;` could be `_T_160 | (sext(_T_51&~T_83)`
Similarly `_T_51 ? _T_83 & _T_161 : _T_161` and `_T_79 ? ~_T_80 & reg_mstatus_sie : reg_mstatus_sie`

---

- [x] This: `io_in1[31] ? 32'hFFFFFFFF : 32'h0;` could be turned into a sext.

---

- [x] This happens a lot in the ALU module:

```
wire [31:0] _T_10 = _T_9 & 32'hFF00FF00;
wire [15:0] _T_14 = _T_10[15:0] | ...
```

could be: `wire [15:0] _T_14 = {_T_9[15:8], 8'h0}`

---

- [x] This is from FPU.fir: ` wbInfo_0_cp <= _T_81 ? (_T_197 ? mem_cp_valid : _T_190) : _T_190;`

Could be: `wbInfo_0_cp <= (_T_81 & _T_197) ? mem_cp_valid : _T_190;`

This happens a bunch of times, e.g. `wbInfo_1_single <= _T_81 ? (_T_200 ? mem_ctrl_single : _T_194) : _T_194;`

---

- [x] This happens in IntToFP from FPU.fir: `wire [32:0] _T_23 = _T_22 ? {1'h0, _T_21} : {_T_21[31], _T_21};`

This could be: `wire [32:0] _T_23 = { _T_22 ? 1'h0 : _T_21[31], _T_21};` which then further simplifies.

---

- [x] This could simplify away the intermediate and (T_26 has multiple uses) from RVCExpander / RocketCore.fir:

```
wire [4:0] _T_26 = _T_25 & 5'hF; 
wire [4:0] _T_28 = _T_26 & 5'h7;
```

 - [x] This could be reassembled into a larger extract and sext (RocketCore.fir):

```
 = {io_in1[0], io_in1[1], io_in1[2], io_in1[3], io_in1[4], ... }

wire _T_12 = _T_11[31];
{_T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12,
 _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_12, _T_11[31:0]};
```
